siteBanner: PITLANE LK · Round 9 — British GP this weekend

news.title: Silverstone Like Never Before: The Impact of the 2026 PU Rules
news.author: Chalitha D.W
news.excerpt: The new 2026 Formula 1 Power Unit rules could change the character of Silverstone dramatically. With battery power running out quickly and fewer chances to recover energy through the fast sections, drivers fear the iconic British circuit may feel slower, less exciting, and very different from the high-speed Silverstone fans are used to.
news.body:
The new 2026 Formula 1 Power Units could make Silverstone slower and less exciting than in previous years. Drivers are worried that one of the calendar’s fastest and most iconic circuits may lose much of its character because of battery limitations and heavy clipping.

Tracks with long full-throttle sections and few braking zones have already exposed the weakness of the new Power Units. Silverstone is expected to be one of the toughest circuits because cars spend a long time at high speed, especially through Copse, Maggots and Becketts, with very few chances to recover energy.

Max Verstappen said he laughed after trying Silverstone on the simulator because the car had almost no battery during the lap. Similar problems were seen at Suzuka, where cars lost up to 50 km/h when they ran out of electric power.

The FIA has also reduced the energy recovery limit in qualifying from 7.5 MJ to 6.5 MJ, making the situation even harder for teams that had already prepared their simulations around the original limit.

Drivers fear that once the battery runs out, the weaker combustion engine will struggle to carry the car through Silverstone’s fastest sections. As a result, corners that were once high-speed challenges could feel more like medium-speed corners.

Carlos Sainz described the simulator experience as shocking, saying the current development is not enough for this type of engine concept. Alex Albon added that the circuit may no longer feel like the high-speed Silverstone everyone knows.

Fernando Alonso also criticised the situation, saying it will be sad for both drivers and fans to see these cars at Silverstone and Spa, two circuits that previously suited the old ground-effect cars perfectly.

si.title: 2026 නව PU නීති යටතේ අපි කවදාවත් නොදුටු Silverstone එකක්
si.excerpt: 2026 Formula 1 නව Power Units සහ battery limitations නිසා Silverstone වැනි වේගවත් circuit එකක සාම්ප්‍රදායික high-speed feeling එක අඩුවිය හැකි බවට රියදුරන් කනස්සල්ල පළ කරති. Battery power ඉක්මනින් අවසන් වීම, ricarica අවස්ථා අඩුවීම සහ FIA විසින් energy recovery limit අඩු කිරීමත් සමඟ, Copse, Maggots සහ Becketts වැනි iconic sections මෙවර පෙර වසරවලට වඩා වෙනස් අත්දැකීමක් ලබාදිය හැක.
si.body:
2026 Formula 1 නව Power Units නිසා Silverstone වගේ වේගවත් සහ ජනප්‍රිය පථයක්, පෙර වසරවලට වඩා මන්දගාමී සහ අඩු රසවත් තරඟයක් බවට පත්විය හැකි බවට රියදුරන් අතර කනස්සල්ලක් මතුව තිබේ.

මෙහි ප්‍රධාන ගැටලුව වන්නේ battery power ඉක්මනින් අවසන් වීම සහ එය නැවත charge කරගැනීමට Silverstone පථයේ අවස්ථා අඩුවීමයි. Copse, Maggots සහ Becketts වැනි වේගවත් කොටස්වලදී කාර් දිගු කාලයක් full throttle යන නිසා battery energy වැඩි වේගයෙන් භාවිතා වේ. නමුත් එය නැවත recover කරගැනීමට ප්‍රමාණවත් braking zones නොමැත.

Max Verstappen පැවසූයේ simulator එකේ Silverstone ධාවනය කළ විට එය සම්පූර්ණයෙන් වෙනස් track එකක් වගේ හැඟුණු බවයි. Lap එකේ බොහෝ කොටස්වල battery power නොමැතිව ධාවනය කළ යුතු වීම නිසා, Silverstone හි සාමාන්‍ය high-speed feeling එක අඩුවී යන බව ඔහු සඳහන් කළේය.

මෙවැනිම ගැටලුවක් Suzuka වලදීත් දැකගත හැකි විය. එහිදී electric power අවසන් වූ විට කාර් වේගයෙන්ම 50 km/h පමණ අහිමි වූ අවස්ථා තිබුණි. Silverstone වලත් එවැනිම තත්ත්වයක් ඇතිවිය හැකි බවට බියක් තිබේ.

මෙයට අමතරව, FIA විසින් qualifying සඳහා energy recovery limit එක 7.5 MJ සිට 6.5 MJ දක්වා අඩු කර ඇත. ඒ නිසා කණ්ඩායම්වලට battery management සහ clipping strategies සකස් කරගැනීම තවත් අමාරු වී ඇත.

රියදුරන්ගේ ප්‍රධාන බිය වන්නේ battery power අවසන් වූ විට, අඩු power එකක් ඇති combustion engine එකට තනිවම කාර් එක ඉදිරියට ගෙන යාමට සිදුවීමයි. එවිට Silverstone හි වේගවත් corners, පෙර වසරවල වගේ අභියෝගාත්මක high-speed corners ලෙස නොව, medium-speed corners වගේ හැඟෙන්න පුළුවන්.

Carlos Sainz මේ simulator experience එක “shocking” ලෙස විස්තර කළ අතර, මෙම නව engine concept එකට වර්තමාන development ප්‍රමාණවත් නොවන බව පැවසීය. Alex Albon ද සඳහන් කළේ Silverstone තවදුරටත් අප දන්නා high-speed circuit එකක් වගේ නොහැඟෙනු ඇති බවයි.

Fernando Alonso ද මෙම තත්ත්වය විවේචනය කරමින්, Silverstone සහ Spa වැනි අතිශය ජනප්‍රිය circuits වලදී මෙම නව කාර් ධාවනය කිරීම රියදුරන්ටත්, ප්‍රේක්ෂකයන්ටත් කණගාටුදායක අත්දැකීමක් විය හැකි බව පැවසීය.

සාරාංශයක් ලෙස, නව Power Units සහ battery limitations නිසා Silverstone හි ඇති සාම්ප්‍රදායික වේගය, රියදුරු අභියෝගය සහ excitement එක මේ වසරේ සැලකිය යුතු ලෙස අඩුවිය හැක.

news.title: Ferrari Prepares Upgrades for the Silverstone Challenge
news.author: Chalitha D.W
news.excerpt: Ferrari heads to Silverstone with new aero upgrades as the team prepares for a difficult weekend. The SF-26 is not perfectly suited to the circuit’s long straights, especially due to engine limitations and drag sensitivity. Ferrari will therefore focus on reducing drag, improving top speed, and managing the tyres better to stay competitive at Silverstone.
news.body:
Silverstone is not expected to be one of Ferrari’s strongest tracks. The British circuit requires low aerodynamic drag and has many medium and high-speed corners, while the SF-26 seems stronger in slower corners. Also, according to Brembo, Silverstone is not demanding on the brakes, with a difficulty rating of only 1 out of 5. This could make energy recovery more difficult, potentially favouring teams using Mercedes Power Units.

Ferrari arrives in Britain with a cautious approach after a disappointing Austrian GP, where the team appeared to drop back to fourth-fastest after the strong win with Lewis Hamilton in Barcelona two weeks earlier.

The team has analysed the setup mistakes made in Austria and will try to apply those lessons at Silverstone. The famous Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel sequence creates very high lateral loads, putting heavy stress on the tyres.

Ferrari must rediscover the strengths of its chassis and aerodynamics. In Austria, too much downforce turned into excessive drag, hurting straight-line speed. For Silverstone, Ferrari’s aerodynamic team is working on reducing drag and will bring an evolved version of its reversible rear wing. When the active wing system opens, there should be a larger gap between the movable flaps and the main profile, helping increase top speed.

A recent image also revealed an interesting detail of Ferrari’s rear wing: with the side cover removed, the hydraulic mechanism that moves the two active flaps can be seen. Ferrari has worked hard to miniaturise this system and make sure the opening and closing times stay within the 0.4-second limit required by the rules.

This is a complex area where Ferrari and Red Bull seem to have found a working solution. McLaren, meanwhile, had planned to test its own version in Austria but had to delay it because the system was not yet functioning properly.

Ferrari has also tested its FTM system with Dino Beganovic at the Red Bull Ring, including runs without the flap that partially blocks the exhaust and redirects gases to create extra rear downforce. This solution costs around 5 to 7 horsepower, so Ferrari is evaluating whether it is still worth using on low-downforce tracks. The FIA has already banned the system for 2027.

si.title: Silverstone අභියෝගයට Ferrari නව Upgrades සමඟ සූදානම්
si.excerpt: Ferrari නව aero upgrades සමඟ Silverstone වෙත පිවිසෙන්නේ අභියෝගාත්මක weekend එකකට සූදානම් වෙමින්ය. SF-26 කාර් එක Silverstone හි දිගු straights වලට පරිපූර්ණව ගැළපෙන්නේ නැත, විශේෂයෙන් engine limitations සහ drag sensitivity නිසා. ඒ නිසා Ferrari මෙහිදී drag අඩු කිරීම, top speed වැඩි කිරීම සහ tyres හොඳින් manage කිරීම මත අවධානය යොමු කරමින් Silverstone හි competitiveව සිටීමට උත්සාහ කරනු ඇත.
si.body:
Silverstone Ferrari කණ්ඩායමට වැඩිය වාසිදායක track එකක් නොවිය හැක. මෙම British circuit එකට අඩු aerodynamic drag එකක් අවශ්‍ය වන අතර, medium සහ high-speed corners බොහෝ ප්‍රමාණයක් තිබේ. නමුත් Ferrari SF-26 කාර් එක වැඩි වශයෙන් ශක්තිමත් වන්නේ slow corners වලදීය. ඒ නිසා Silverstone හිදී Ferrariට තරමක් අභියෝගාත්මක weekend එකක් විය හැක.

Brembo ඉංජිනේරුවන්ට අනුව, Silverstone brake system එකට වැඩි stress එකක් දෙන track එකක් නොවේ. Difficulty level එක 1 සිට 5 දක්වා scale එකක 1ක් පමණි. මෙය battery energy recovery එකටත් බලපායි, මන්ද brake zones අඩු වූ විට battery එක නැවත charge කරගැනීම අමාරු වේ. ඒ නිසා Mercedes Power Unit භාවිතා කරන කණ්ඩායම්වලට මෙහි වාසියක් ලැබිය හැක.

Ferrari British GP එකට පිවිසෙන්නේ තරමක් ප්‍රවේශම් සහගත ආකාරයකින්ය. Austria GP එකේදී team එක බලාපොරොත්තු වූ performance එක ලබාගැනීමට නොහැකි වූ අතර, setup තෝරාගැනීමේදී සිදු වූ වැරදි Ferrari විසින් දැන් විශ්ලේෂණය කරමින් සිටී.

Silverstone හි Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel වැනි ඉතා වේගවත් corner sequence එක tyres වලට විශාල lateral load එකක් ලබා දෙයි. ඒ නිසා tyre management මෙහි ඉතා වැදගත් වනු ඇත.

Ferrariට මෙහිදී තම chassis සහ aerodynamics වල ශක්තිය නැවත පෙන්විය යුතුය. Austria වලදී වැඩි downforce එකක් තෝරාගැනීම නිසා straight-line speed අඩු වී drag වැඩි විය. ඒ නිසා Silverstone සඳහා Ferrari aerodynamic team එක drag අඩු කිරීමේ වැඩකටයුතු කරමින් සිටී.

මෙම weekend එකේදී Ferrari විසින් තම reversible rear wing එකේ නව version එකක් භාවිතා කිරීමට සූදානම් වේ. Active wing system එක open වන විට movable flaps දෙක සහ main profile එක අතර gap එක වැඩි වනු ඇත. එමඟින් top speed වැඩි කරගැනීමට Ferrari බලාපොරොත්තු වේ.

Social media සහ motorsport websites වල පළ වූ තොරතුරු අනුව, Ferrari rear wing එකේ රසවත් තාක්ෂණික විස්තරයක් දැකගත හැකි විය. Side cover එක ඉවත් කර තිබූ නිසා, active flaps දෙක චලනය කරන hydraulic mechanism එක පැහැදිලිව හඳුනාගත හැකි වි Ferrari engineers මේ system එක කුඩා කරගැනීමටත්, opening සහ closing time එක නීති අනුව 0.4 seconds ඇතුළත තබාගැනීමටත් වැඩි අවධානයක් දී ඇත.

මෙය ඉතා සංකීර්ණ තාක්ෂණික වැඩකි. Ferrari සහ Red Bull මේ සම්බන්ධයෙන් හොඳ විසඳුමක් සොයාගෙන ඇති බව පෙනේ. McLaren තම solution එක Austria වලදී test කිරීමට සූදානම් වූ නමුත්, system එක තවම හොඳින් ක්‍රියා නොකළ නිසා එය පසුදමා ඇත.

Ferrari තවදුරටත් FTM system එක ගැනත් පරීක්ෂණ කරමින් සිටී. Dino Beganovic සමඟ Red Bull Ring හිදී, exhaust gas flow එක භාවිතා කර rear downforce වැඩි කරන flap එක නොමැතිව ද runs කර ඇත. මෙම system එක horsepower 5 සිට 7 දක්වා power loss එකක් ඇති කළ හැකි බැවින්, low-downforce tracks වලදී එය භාවිතා කිරීම වටිනාද යන්න Ferrari දැන් ඇගයීමට ලක් කරයි.

සාරාංශයක් ලෙස, Silverstone Ferrariට පහසු track එකක් නොවිය හැක. නමුත් drag අඩු කිරීම, rear wing development සහ tyre management හරහා Ferrariට performance එක නැවත හොඳ මට්ටමකට ගෙන ඒමට අවස්ථාවක් තිබේ.

about.title: About PITLANE LK
about.intro:
**PITLANE LK** is an independent Formula 1-related news and information platform created especially for **Formula 1 fans in Sri Lanka** and Sri Lankan F1 supporters around the world.

Built from a genuine passion for Formula 1, PITLANE LK was created to bring the sport closer to the growing F1 community in Sri Lanka. This platform is for Sri Lankan fans who love racing, follow the teams and drivers, and want a dedicated space to stay updated with the world of Formula 1.

Our mission is to make Formula 1 easier, more exciting, and more accessible for fans in Sri Lanka. Through independent articles, commentary, race-weekend updates, championship information, season calendars, and statistics, PITLANE LK helps Sri Lankan F1 fans follow the sport in one simple place.

We also support both **English and Sinhala articles**, helping fans in Sri Lanka enjoy Formula 1-related content in the language they are most comfortable with.

sections.title: What We Do
sections.body:
PITLANE LK shares independent Formula 1-related news, commentary, race-weekend updates, championship information, team and driver stories, season calendars, and season statistics.

Our content is created for informational, editorial, commentary, and fan-community purposes. Whether you are a long-time Formula 1 follower or a new fan discovering the sport, PITLANE LK aims to make F1 easier to follow for the Sri Lankan community.

sections.title: Our Independence
sections.body:
PITLANE LK is an independent platform and is **not affiliated with, endorsed by, sponsored by, or officially connected to Formula 1, Formula One Management, the FIA, or any Formula 1 team**.

All team names, driver names, race names, championship references, logos, images, and related Formula 1 references are used only for identification, news reporting, commentary, editorial, and informational purposes.

PITLANE LK does not claim ownership of any third-party trademarks, logos, images, or intellectual property belonging to Formula 1, the FIA, teams, drivers, sponsors, or other rights holders.

sections.title: Content Concerns
sections.body:
PITLANE LK aims to respect the rights of Formula 1, the FIA, teams, drivers, photographers, media owners, sponsors, and all other rights holders.

If you believe that any content published on PITLANE LK infringes your rights, has been used incorrectly, or should be reviewed, please contact us through the {contact} form on our homepage.

We will review the matter and, where appropriate, update, credit, modify, or remove the relevant content.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.intro:
PITLANE LK is an independent Formula 1 news and information website. We are not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to Formula 1, the FIA, Formula One Management, Liberty Media, or any F1 team.

Formula 1, FIA Formula One World Championship, Grand Prix and related marks are trademarks of their respective owners. All team and driver names are used for identification and reporting purposes only.

sections.title: Editorial content
sections.body: News, standings, and statistics on this site are for general information. While we aim to keep content accurate and up to date, we do not guarantee completeness or correctness. Always refer to official sources for confirmed results and regulations.

sections.title: No professional advice
sections.body: Nothing on this site constitutes professional, legal, or financial advice.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: PITLANE LK ("we", "us") respects your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect and how we use it.

sections.title: Information we collect
sections.body:
**Contact form:** If you use our Contact page, you may provide your name, email, subject, and message. This is stored locally in your browser (localStorage) on the device you use to submit the form.

**Site preferences:** Your article language choice (English / Sinhala) is saved in your browser so the site remembers your selection.

**Admin access:** If you use the private admin panel, a session flag is stored in your browser.

sections.title: How we use information
sections.body: Contact messages are intended for the site operator to read and respond. We do not sell personal data. We do not currently send data to a remote server as part of normal site operation.

sections.title: Third parties
sections.body: If we add analytics, advertising, or embedded content from third parties in future, this policy will be updated. Third-party services may collect data under their own policies.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: You can clear site data at any time by clearing your browser's local storage for this website. To ask about data we hold, contact us via the {contact} page.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Questions about this policy: use the contact form on our homepage or email the address listed on our Contact page.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This site uses minimal browser storage. We do not currently use advertising or analytics cookies.

sections.title: What we use today
sections.body: **Local storage** — to save your language preference, site content (admin), and contact form messages in your browser. This is not a cookie but works similarly for storing small amounts of data on your device.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body: We do not set marketing or tracking cookies at this time. If that changes (for example Google Analytics or ad partners), we will update this page and, where required, ask for your consent.

sections.title: Managing storage
sections.body: You can delete local storage and cookies through your browser settings. Clearing site data will reset language preference and any locally saved contact messages on your device.

sections.title: More information
sections.body: See our {privacy} for how we handle personal information.

advertise.placementsDesc: Standard formats shown below — we supply the slots, you supply the creative (or we can help design).
advertise.packagesDesc: Flexible options for local brands, automotive, hospitality, and international partners targeting Sri Lankan F1 fans.
advertise.footerNote: Ready to start? {contact} with your company name, preferred package, and campaign dates.
